2604.21928.title: Evaluation of Automatic Speech Recognition Using Generative Large Language Models
2604.21928.paper_summary: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) is traditionally evaluated using Word Error Rate (WER), a metric that is insensitive to meaning. Embedding-based semantic metrics are better correlated with human perception, but decoder-based Large Language Models (LLMs) remain underexplored for this task. This paper evaluates their relevance through three approaches: (1) selecting the best hypothesis between two candidates, (2) computing semantic distance using generative embeddings, and (3) qualitative classification of errors. On the HATS dataset, the best LLMs achieve 92--94\% agreement with human annotators for hypothesis selection, compared to 63\% for WER, also outperforming semantic metrics. Embeddings from decoder-based LLMs show performance comparable to encoder models. Finally, LLMs offer a promising direction for interpretable and semantic ASR evaluation.
2604.21928.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音辨識（ASR）傳統上是使用字詞錯誤率（WER）來評估的，這是一個對意義不敏感的指標。基於嵌入的語義指標與人類感知的相關性更高，但基於解碼器的大型語言模型（LLMs）在這一任務中仍然未被充分探索。本文通過三種方法評估它們的相關性：（1）在兩個候選者之間選擇最佳假設，（2）使用生成嵌入計算語義距離，以及（3）對錯誤進行質性分類。在HATS數據集上，最佳的LLMs在假設選擇上與人類標註者達到92--94\%的協議，而WER則為63\%，同時在語義指標上也表現優於它們。來自基於解碼器的LLMs的嵌入表現與編碼器模型相當。最後，LLMs為可解釋和語義的ASR評估提供了一個有前景的方向。
2604.21928.author: Thibault Bañeras-Roux et.al.
2604.21928.authors: Thibault Bañeras-Roux, Shashi Kumar, Driss Khalil, Sergio Burdisso, Petr Motlicek, Shiran Liu, Mickael Rouvier, Jane Wottawa, Richard Dufour
